subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Discovery of Exoplanets
description: One-minute skill: The Discovery of Exoplanets.

hook.text: For thousands of years, we wondered if other stars have planets. In 1992, we finally found proof.

buildup.text: The first exoplanets were found orbiting a pulsar—a dead star. Nobody expected planets around a stellar corpse.

discovery.text: In 1995, Michel Mayor and Didier Queloz found 51 Pegasi b—the first exoplanet around a Sun-like star.

twist.text: 51 Pegasi b was a 'hot Jupiter'—a gas giant orbiting closer than Mercury. It defied all formation models.

climax.text: Today, over 5,600 exoplanets are confirmed. The Kepler telescope alone found thousands by watching stars dim.

punchline.text: From zero to five thousand in thirty years. Planets are everywhere.

quiz.question: What was unusual about 51 Pegasi b, the first exoplanet found around a Sun-like star?
options: It was a gas giant orbiting extremely close to its star—a 'hot Jupiter'
It was a rocky planet in the habitable zone
